content.content:
1	# AXI Demultiplexer
2	
3	`axi_demux` splits one AXI connection into multiple ones.  It implements the full AXI4 specification plus atomic operations (ATOPs) from AXI5.
4	
5	
6	## Design Overview
7	
8	The demultiplexer has one *slave port* and a configurable number of *master ports*.  A block diagram is shown below:
9	
10	![Block diagram of the AXI demultiplexer](axi_demux.png "Block diagram of the AXI demultiplexer")
11	
12	The AW and AR channels each have a *select* input, to determine the master port to which they are sent.  The select can, for example, be driven by an (external) address decoder to map address ranges to different AXI slaves.
13	
14	Beats on the W channel are routed by demultiplexer according to the selection for the corresponding AW beat.  This relies on the AXI property that W bursts must be sent in the same order as AW beats and beats from different W bursts may not be interleaved.
15	
16	Beats on the B and R channel are multiplexed from the master ports to the slave port with a round-robin arbitration tree.
17	
18	
19	## Configuration
20	
21	This demultiplexer is configured through the parameters listed in the following table:
22	
23	| Name                 | Type               | Definition |
24	|:---------------------|:-------------------|:-----------|
25	| `AxiIdWidth`         | `int unsigned`     | The AXI ID width (of all ports). |
26	| `NoMstPorts`         | `int unsigned`     | The number of AXI master ports of the demultiplexer (in other words, how many AXI slave modules can be attached). |
27	| `MaxTrans`           | `int unsigned`     | The slave port can have at most this many transactions [in flight](../doc#in-flight). |
28	| `AxiLookBits`        | `int unsigned`     | The number of ID bits (starting at the least significant) the demultiplexer uses to determine the uniqueness of an AXI ID (see section *Ordering and Stalls* below).  This value has to be less or equal than `AxiIdWidth`. |
29	| `UniqueIds`          | `bit`              | If you can guarantee that the ID of each transaction is always unique among all in-flight transactions in the same direction, setting this parameter to `1'b1` simplifies the demultiplexer (see section *Ordering and Stalls* below).  Defaults to `1'b0`. |
30	| `FallThrough`        | `bit`              | Routing decisions on the AW channel fall through to the W channel.  Enabling this allows the demultiplexer to accept a W beat in the same cycle as the corresponding AW beat, but it increases the combinatorial path of the W channel with logic from `slv_aw_select_i`. |
31	| `SpillXX`            | `bit`              | Inserts one spill register on the respective channel (AW, W, B, AR, and R) before the demultiplexer. |
32	
33	The other parameters are types to define the ports of the demultiplexer.  The `_*chan_t` types must be bound in accordance to the configuration using the `AXI_TYPEDEF` macros defined in `axi/typedef.svh`.
34	
35	### Pipelining and Latency
36	
37	The `SpillXX` parameters allow to insert spill register before each channel of the demultiplexer.  Spill registers cut all combinatorial paths of a channel (i.e., both payload and handshake).  Thus, they add one cycle of latency per channel but do not impair throughput.
38	
39	If all `SpillXX` and `FallThrough` are disabled, all paths through this multiplexer are combinatorial (i.e., have zero sequential latency).
40	
41	
42	## Ports
43	
44	| Name                              | Description |
45	|:----------------------------------|:------------|
46	| `clk_i`                           | Clock to which all other signals (except `rst_ni`) are synchronous. |
47	| `rst_ni`                          | Reset, asynchronous, active-low. |
48	| `test_i`                          | Test mode enable (active-high). |
49	| `slv_*` (except `slv_*_select_i`) | Single slave port of the demultiplexer. |
50	| `slv_{aw,ar}_select_i`            | Index of the master port to which a write or read, respectively, is demultiplexed.  This signal must be stable while a handshake on the AW respectively AR channel is [pending](../doc#pending). |
51	| `mst_*`                           | Array of master ports of the demultiplexer.  The array index of each port is the index of the master port. |
52	
53	
54	## Ordering and Stalls
55	
56	When the demultiplexer receives two transactions with the same ID and direction (i.e., both read or both write) but targeting two different master ports, it will not accept the second transaction until the first has completed.  During this time, the demultiplexer stalls the AR or AW channel, respectively.  To determine whether two transactions have the same ID, the `AxiLookBits` least-significant bits are compared.  That parameter can be set to the full `AxiIdWidth` to avoid false ID conflicts, or it can be set to a lower value to reduce area and delay at the cost of more false conflicts.
57	
58	The reason for this behavior are AXI ordering constraints, see the [documentation of the crossbar](axi_xbar.md#ordering-and-stalls) for details.
59	
60	There are use cases that do not require the demultiplexer to keep track of and enforce this ordering, and the `UniqueIds` parameter can be set to specialize the demultiplexer for these cases:
61	`UniqueIds` may be set to `1'b1` if and only if
62	- each transaction has an ID that is unique among all in-flight transactions in the same direction;
63	- or for any ID, all transactions with that ID target the same master port as all other in-flight transactions with the same ID and direction;
64	- or both.
65	
66	Setting the `UniqueIds` parameter to `1'b1` when those conditions are not always met leads to undefined behavior.
67	
68	Setting the `UniqueIds` parameter to `1'b1` reduces the area complexity of the demultiplexer from `O(2^I)` to `O(I)`, where `I` is the ID width.
69	
70	### Implementation
71	
72	`2 * 2^AxiLookBits` counters track the number of [in-flight](../doc#in-flight) transactions.  That is, for each ID in the (potentially) reduced set of IDs of `AxiLookBits` bits, there is one counter for write transactions and one for read transactions.  Each counter can count up to (and including) `MaxTrans`, and there is a register that holds the index of the master port to which a counter is assigned.
73	
74	When the demultiplexer gets an AW or an AR, it indexes the counters with the AXI ID.  If the indexed counter has a value greater than zero and its master port index register is not equal to the index to which the AW or AR is to be sent, a transaction with the same direction and ID is already in flight to another master port.  The demultiplexer then stalls the AW or AR.  In all other cases, the demultiplexer forwards the AW or AR, increments the value of the indexed counter, and sets the master port index of the counter.  A counter is decremented upon a handshake a B respectively last R beat at a slave port.
75	
76	W beats are routed to the master port defined by the value of `slv_aw_select_i` for the corresponding AW.  As the order of the W bursts is given by the order of the AWs, the select signals are stored in a FIFO queue.  This FIFO is pushed upon a handshake on the AW slave channel and popped upon a handshake of the last W beat of a burst on a W master channel.
77	
78	
79	## Atomic Transactions
80	
81	The demultiplexer also supports AXI atomic transactions (initiated by an AW with `atop` field not equal to zero).  A part of AXI atomic transactions, namely atomic loads, require a response on the B *and* the R channel.
82	
83	### Implementation
84	
85	Atomic loads introduce a dependency between the read and write channels that otherwise does not exist in AXI.  In this demultiplexer, the ID counters of the read channel must be aware of R beats without a corresponding AR.  Otherwise, they would underflow upon atomic loads.  To prevent this, the AW channel of the demultiplexer can "inject" the ID of an atomic load to the ID counter of the AR channel.  This is possible because atomic transactions must have an ID that is unique with respect to *all* other transactions (i.e., reads *and* writes) currently in flight.
86	
87	As only a part of the AXI ID is compared to determine whether two transactions have the same ID (see section *Ordering and Stalls*), atomic loads can lead to additional false conflict stalls on the read channel.  However, atomic transactions are short bursts and thus usually complete relatively fast, so this should not reduce throughput under non-degenerate conditions.
88

file.content:
# AXI Demultiplexer

`axi_demux` splits one AXI connection into multiple ones.  It implements the full AXI4 specification plus atomic operations (ATOPs) from AXI5.


## Design Overview

The demultiplexer has one *slave port* and a configurable number of *master ports*.  A block diagram is shown below:

![Block diagram of the AXI demultiplexer](axi_demux.png "Block diagram of the AXI demultiplexer")

The AW and AR channels each have a *select* input, to determine the master port to which they are sent.  The select can, for example, be driven by an (external) address decoder to map address ranges to different AXI slaves.

Beats on the W channel are routed by demultiplexer according to the selection for the corresponding AW beat.  This relies on the AXI property that W bursts must be sent in the same order as AW beats and beats from different W bursts may not be interleaved.

Beats on the B and R channel are multiplexed from the master ports to the slave port with a round-robin arbitration tree.


## Configuration

This demultiplexer is configured through the parameters listed in the following table:

| Name                 | Type               | Definition |
|:---------------------|:-------------------|:-----------|
| `AxiIdWidth`         | `int unsigned`     | The AXI ID width (of all ports). |
| `NoMstPorts`         | `int unsigned`     | The number of AXI master ports of the demultiplexer (in other words, how many AXI slave modules can be attached). |
| `MaxTrans`           | `int unsigned`     | The slave port can have at most this many transactions [in flight](../doc#in-flight). |
| `AxiLookBits`        | `int unsigned`     | The number of ID bits (starting at the least significant) the demultiplexer uses to determine the uniqueness of an AXI ID (see section *Ordering and Stalls* below).  This value has to be less or equal than `AxiIdWidth`. |
| `UniqueIds`          | `bit`              | If you can guarantee that the ID of each transaction is always unique among all in-flight transactions in the same direction, setting this parameter to `1'b1` simplifies the demultiplexer (see section *Ordering and Stalls* below).  Defaults to `1'b0`. |
| `FallThrough`        | `bit`              | Routing decisions on the AW channel fall through to the W channel.  Enabling this allows the demultiplexer to accept a W beat in the same cycle as the corresponding AW beat, but it increases the combinatorial path of the W channel with logic from `slv_aw_select_i`. |
| `SpillXX`            | `bit`              | Inserts one spill register on the respective channel (AW, W, B, AR, and R) before the demultiplexer. |

The other parameters are types to define the ports of the demultiplexer.  The `_*chan_t` types must be bound in accordance to the configuration using the `AXI_TYPEDEF` macros defined in `axi/typedef.svh`.

### Pipelining and Latency

The `SpillXX` parameters allow to insert spill register before each channel of the demultiplexer.  Spill registers cut all combinatorial paths of a channel (i.e., both payload and handshake).  Thus, they add one cycle of latency per channel but do not impair throughput.

If all `SpillXX` and `FallThrough` are disabled, all paths through this multiplexer are combinatorial (i.e., have zero sequential latency).


## Ports

| Name                              | Description |
|:----------------------------------|:------------|
| `clk_i`                           | Clock to which all other signals (except `rst_ni`) are synchronous. |
| `rst_ni`                          | Reset, asynchronous, active-low. |
| `test_i`                          | Test mode enable (active-high). |
| `slv_*` (except `slv_*_select_i`) | Single slave port of the demultiplexer. |
| `slv_{aw,ar}_select_i`            | Index of the master port to which a write or read, respectively, is demultiplexed.  This signal must be stable while a handshake on the AW respectively AR channel is [pending](../doc#pending). |
| `mst_*`                           | Array of master ports of the demultiplexer.  The array index of each port is the index of the master port. |


## Ordering and Stalls

When the demultiplexer receives two transactions with the same ID and direction (i.e., both read or both write) but targeting two different master ports, it will not accept the second transaction until the first has completed.  During this time, the demultiplexer stalls the AR or AW channel, respectively.  To determine whether two transactions have the same ID, the `AxiLookBits` least-significant bits are compared.  That parameter can be set to the full `AxiIdWidth` to avoid false ID conflicts, or it can be set to a lower value to reduce area and delay at the cost of more false conflicts.

The reason for this behavior are AXI ordering constraints, see the [documentation of the crossbar](axi_xbar.md#ordering-and-stalls) for details.

There are use cases that do not require the demultiplexer to keep track of and enforce this ordering, and the `UniqueIds` parameter can be set to specialize the demultiplexer for these cases:
`UniqueIds` may be set to `1'b1` if and only if
- each transaction has an ID that is unique among all in-flight transactions in the same direction;
- or for any ID, all transactions with that ID target the same master port as all other in-flight transactions with the same ID and direction;
- or both.

Setting the `UniqueIds` parameter to `1'b1` when those conditions are not always met leads to undefined behavior.

Setting the `UniqueIds` parameter to `1'b1` reduces the area complexity of the demultiplexer from `O(2^I)` to `O(I)`, where `I` is the ID width.

### Implementation

`2 * 2^AxiLookBits` counters track the number of [in-flight](../doc#in-flight) transactions.  That is, for each ID in the (potentially) reduced set of IDs of `AxiLookBits` bits, there is one counter for write transactions and one for read transactions.  Each counter can count up to (and including) `MaxTrans`, and there is a register that holds the index of the master port to which a counter is assigned.

When the demultiplexer gets an AW or an AR, it indexes the counters with the AXI ID.  If the indexed counter has a value greater than zero and its master port index register is not equal to the index to which the AW or AR is to be sent, a transaction with the same direction and ID is already in flight to another master port.  The demultiplexer then stalls the AW or AR.  In all other cases, the demultiplexer forwards the AW or AR, increments the value of the indexed counter, and sets the master port index of the counter.  A counter is decremented upon a handshake a B respectively last R beat at a slave port.

W beats are routed to the master port defined by the value of `slv_aw_select_i` for the corresponding AW.  As the order of the W bursts is given by the order of the AWs, the select signals are stored in a FIFO queue.  This FIFO is pushed upon a handshake on the AW slave channel and popped upon a handshake of the last W beat of a burst on a W master channel.


## Atomic Transactions

The demultiplexer also supports AXI atomic transactions (initiated by an AW with `atop` field not equal to zero).  A part of AXI atomic transactions, namely atomic loads, require a response on the B *and* the R channel.

### Implementation

Atomic loads introduce a dependency between the read and write channels that otherwise does not exist in AXI.  In this demultiplexer, the ID counters of the read channel must be aware of R beats without a corresponding AR.  Otherwise, they would underflow upon atomic loads.  To prevent this, the AW channel of the demultiplexer can "inject" the ID of an atomic load to the ID counter of the AR channel.  This is possible because atomic transactions must have an ID that is unique with respect to *all* other transactions (i.e., reads *and* writes) currently in flight.

As only a part of the AXI ID is compared to determine whether two transactions have the same ID (see section *Ordering and Stalls*), atomic loads can lead to additional false conflict stalls on the read channel.  However, atomic transactions are short bursts and thus usually complete relatively fast, so this should not reduce throughput under non-degenerate conditions.

1	// Copyright (c) 2019 ETH Zurich, University of Bologna
2	//
3	// Copyright and related rights are licensed under the Solderpad Hardware

5	// compliance with the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
6	// http://solderpad.org/licenses/SHL-0.51. Unless required by applicable law
7	// or agreed to in writing, software, hardware and materials distributed under
8	// this License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS, WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR
9	// CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. See the License for the
10	// specific language governing permissions and limitations under the License.
11	//
12	// Authors:
13	// - Wolfgang Roenninger <wroennin@iis.ee.ethz.ch>
14	// - Andreas Kurth <akurth@iis.ee.ethz.ch>
15	
16	// AXI Multiplexer: This module multiplexes the AXI4 slave ports down to one master port.
17	// The AXI IDs from the slave ports get extended with the respective slave port index.
18	// The extension width can be calculated with `$clog2(NoSlvPorts)`. This means the AXI
19	// ID for the master port has to be this `$clog2(NoSlvPorts)` wider than the ID for the
20	// slave ports.
21	// Responses are switched based on these bits. For example, with 4 slave ports
22	// a response with ID `6'b100110` will be forwarded to slave port 2 (`2'b10`).
23	
24	// register macros
25	`include "common_cells/assertions.svh"
26	`include "common_cells/registers.svh"
27	
28	module axi_mux #(
29	  // AXI parameter and channel types
30	  parameter int unsigned SlvAxiIDWidth = 32'd0, // AXI ID width, slave ports
31	  parameter type         slv_aw_chan_t = logic, // AW Channel Type, slave ports
32	  parameter type         mst_aw_chan_t = logic, // AW Channel Type, master port
33	  parameter type         w_chan_t      = logic, //  W Channel Type, all ports
34	  parameter type         slv_b_chan_t  = logic, //  B Channel Type, slave ports
35	  parameter type         mst_b_chan_t  = logic, //  B Channel Type, master port
36	  parameter type         slv_ar_chan_t = logic, // AR Channel Type, slave ports
37	  parameter type         mst_ar_chan_t = logic, // AR Channel Type, master port
38	  parameter type         slv_r_chan_t  = logic, //  R Channel Type, slave ports
39	  parameter type         mst_r_chan_t  = logic, //  R Channel Type, master port
40	  parameter type         slv_req_t     = logic, // Slave port request type
41	  parameter type         slv_resp_t    = logic, // Slave port response type
42	  parameter type         mst_req_t     = logic, // Master ports request type
43	  parameter type         mst_resp_t    = logic, // Master ports response type
44	  parameter int unsigned NoSlvPorts    = 32'd0, // Number of slave ports
45	  // Maximum number of outstanding transactions per write
46	  parameter int unsigned MaxWTrans     = 32'd8,
47	  // If enabled, this multiplexer is purely combinatorial
48	  parameter bit          FallThrough   = 1'b0,
49	  // add spill register on write master ports, adds a cycle latency on write channels
50	  parameter bit          SpillAw       = 1'b1,
51	  parameter bit          SpillW        = 1'b0,
52	  parameter bit          SpillB        = 1'b0,
53	  // add spill register on read master ports, adds a cycle latency on read channels
54	  parameter bit          SpillAr       = 1'b1,
55	  parameter bit          SpillR        = 1'b0
56	) (
57	  input  logic                       clk_i,    // Clock
58	  input  logic                       rst_ni,   // Asynchronous reset active low
59	  input  logic                       test_i,   // Test Mode enable
60	  // slave ports (AXI inputs), connect master modules here
61	  input  slv_req_t  [NoSlvPorts-1:0] slv_reqs_i,
62	  output slv_resp_t [NoSlvPorts-1:0] slv_resps_o,
63	  // master port (AXI outputs), connect slave modules here
64	  output mst_req_t                   mst_req_o,
65	  input  mst_resp_t                  mst_resp_i
66	);
67	
68	  localparam int unsigned MstIdxBits    = $clog2(NoSlvPorts);
69	  localparam int unsigned MstAxiIDWidth = SlvAxiIDWidth + MstIdxBits;
70	
71	  // pass through if only one slave port
72	  if (NoSlvPorts == 32'h1) begin : gen_no_mux
73	    spill_register #(
74	      .T       ( mst_aw_chan_t ),
75	      .Bypass  ( ~SpillAw      )
76	    ) i_aw_spill_reg (
77	      .clk_i   ( clk_i                    ),
78	      .rst_ni  ( rst_ni                   ),
79	      .valid_i ( slv_reqs_i[0].aw_valid   ),
80	      .ready_o ( slv_resps_o[0].aw_ready  ),
81	      .data_i  ( slv_reqs_i[0].aw         ),
82	      .valid_o ( mst_req_o.aw_valid       ),
83	      .ready_i ( mst_resp_i.aw_ready      ),
84	      .data_o  ( mst_req_o.aw             )
85	    );
86	    spill_register #(
87	      .T       ( w_chan_t ),
88	      .Bypass  ( ~SpillW  )
89	    ) i_w_spill_reg (
90	      .clk_i   ( clk_i                   ),
91	      .rst_ni  ( rst_ni                  ),
92	      .valid_i ( slv_reqs_i[0].w_valid   ),
93	      .ready_o ( slv_resps_o[0].w_ready  ),
94	      .data_i  ( slv_reqs_i[0].w         ),
95	      .valid_o ( mst_req_o.w_valid       ),
96	      .ready_i ( mst_resp_i.w_ready      ),
97	      .data_o  ( mst_req_o.w             )
98	    );
99	    spill_register #(
100	      .T       ( mst_b_chan_t ),
101	      .Bypass  ( ~SpillB      )
102	    ) i_b_spill_reg (
103	      .clk_i   ( clk_i                  ),
104	      .rst_ni  ( rst_ni                 ),
105	      .valid_i ( mst_resp_i.b_valid     ),
106	      .ready_o ( mst_req_o.b_ready      ),
107	      .data_i  ( mst_resp_i.b           ),
108	      .valid_o ( slv_resps_o[0].b_valid ),
109	      .ready_i ( slv_reqs_i[0].b_ready  ),
110	      .data_o  ( slv_resps_o[0].b       )
111	    );
112	    spill_register #(
113	      .T       ( mst_ar_chan_t ),
114	      .Bypass  ( ~SpillAr      )
115	    ) i_ar_spill_reg (
116	      .clk_i   ( clk_i                    ),
117	      .rst_ni  ( rst_ni                   ),
118	      .valid_i ( slv_reqs_i[0].ar_valid   ),
119	      .ready_o ( slv_resps_o[0].ar_ready  ),
120	      .data_i  ( slv_reqs_i[0].ar         ),
121	      .valid_o ( mst_req_o.ar_valid       ),
122	      .ready_i ( mst_resp_i.ar_ready      ),
123	      .data_o  ( mst_req_o.ar             )
124	    );
125	    spill_register #(
126	      .T       ( mst_r_chan_t ),
127	      .Bypass  ( ~SpillR      )
128	    ) i_r_spill_reg (
129	      .clk_i   ( clk_i                  ),
130	      .rst_ni  ( rst_ni                 ),
131	      .valid_i ( mst_resp_i.r_valid     ),
132	      .ready_o ( mst_req_o.r_ready      ),
133	      .data_i  ( mst_resp_i.r           ),
134	      .valid_o ( slv_resps_o[0].r_valid ),
135	      .ready_i ( slv_reqs_i[0].r_ready  ),
136	      .data_o  ( slv_resps_o[0].r       )
137	    );
138	// Validate parameters.
139	// pragma translate_off
140	    `ASSERT_INIT(CorrectIdWidthSlvAw, $bits(slv_reqs_i[0].aw.id) == SlvAxiIDWidth)
141	    `ASSERT_INIT(CorrectIdWidthSlvB, $bits(slv_resps_o[0].b.id) == SlvAxiIDWidth)
142	    `ASSERT_INIT(CorrectIdWidthSlvAr, $bits(slv_reqs_i[0].ar.id) == SlvAxiIDWidth)
143	    `ASSERT_INIT(CorrectIdWidthSlvR, $bits(slv_resps_o[0].r.id) == SlvAxiIDWidth)
144	    `ASSERT_INIT(CorrectIdWidthMstAw, $bits(mst_req_o.aw.id) == SlvAxiIDWidth)
145	    `ASSERT_INIT(CorrectIdWidthMstB, $bits(mst_resp_i.b.id) == SlvAxiIDWidth)
146	    `ASSERT_INIT(CorrectIdWidthMstAr, $bits(mst_req_o.ar.id) == SlvAxiIDWidth)
147	    `ASSERT_INIT(CorrectIdWidthMstR, $bits(mst_resp_i.r.id) == SlvAxiIDWidth)
148	// pragma translate_on
149	
150	  // other non degenerate cases
151	  end else begin : gen_mux
152	
153	    typedef logic [MstIdxBits-1:0] switch_id_t;
154	
155	    // AXI channels between the ID prepend unit and the rest of the multiplexer
156	    mst_aw_chan_t [NoSlvPorts-1:0] slv_aw_chans;
157	    logic         [NoSlvPorts-1:0] slv_aw_valids, slv_aw_readies;
158	    w_chan_t      [NoSlvPorts-1:0] slv_w_chans;
159	    logic         [NoSlvPorts-1:0] slv_w_valids,  slv_w_readies;
160	    mst_b_chan_t  [NoSlvPorts-1:0] slv_b_chans;
161	    logic         [NoSlvPorts-1:0] slv_b_valids,  slv_b_readies;
162	    mst_ar_chan_t [NoSlvPorts-1:0] slv_ar_chans;
163	    logic         [NoSlvPorts-1:0] slv_ar_valids, slv_ar_readies;
164	    mst_r_chan_t  [NoSlvPorts-1:0] slv_r_chans;
165	    logic         [NoSlvPorts-1:0] slv_r_valids,  slv_r_readies;
166	
167	    // These signals are all ID prepended
168	    // AW channel
169	    mst_aw_chan_t   mst_aw_chan;
170	    logic           mst_aw_valid, mst_aw_ready;
171	
172	    // AW master handshake internal, so that we are able to stall, if w_fifo is full
173	    logic           aw_valid,     aw_ready;
174	
175	    // FF to lock the AW valid signal, when a new arbitration decision is made the decision
176	    // gets pushed into the W FIFO, when it now stalls prevent subsequent pushing
177	    // This FF removes AW to W dependency
178	    logic           lock_aw_valid_d, lock_aw_valid_q;
179	    logic           load_aw_lock;
180	
181	    // signals for the FIFO that holds the last switching decision of the AW channel
182	    logic           w_fifo_full,  w_fifo_empty;
183	    logic           w_fifo_push,  w_fifo_pop;
184	    switch_id_t     w_fifo_data;
185	
186	    // W channel spill reg
187	    w_chan_t        mst_w_chan;
188	    logic           mst_w_valid,  mst_w_ready;
189	
190	    // master ID in the b_id
191	    switch_id_t     switch_b_id;
192	
193	    // B channel spill reg
194	    mst_b_chan_t    mst_b_chan;
195	    logic           mst_b_valid;
196	
197	    // AR channel for when spill is enabled
198	    mst_ar_chan_t   mst_ar_chan;
199	    logic           ar_valid,     ar_ready;
200	
201	    // master ID in the r_id
202	    switch_id_t     switch_r_id;
203	
204	    // R channel spill reg
205	    mst_r_chan_t    mst_r_chan;
206	    logic           mst_r_valid;
207	
208	    //--------------------------------------
209	    // ID prepend for all slave ports
210	    //--------------------------------------
211	    for (genvar i = 0; i < NoSlvPorts; i++) begin : gen_id_prepend
212	      axi_id_prepend #(
213	        .NoBus            ( 32'd1               ), // one AXI bus per slave port
214	        .AxiIdWidthSlvPort( SlvAxiIDWidth       ),
215	        .AxiIdWidthMstPort( MstAxiIDWidth       ),
216	        .slv_aw_chan_t    ( slv_aw_chan_t       ),
217	        .slv_w_chan_t     ( w_chan_t            ),
218	        .slv_b_chan_t     ( slv_b_chan_t        ),
219	        .slv_ar_chan_t    ( slv_ar_chan_t       ),
220	        .slv_r_chan_t     ( slv_r_chan_t        ),
221	        .mst_aw_chan_t    ( mst_aw_chan_t       ),
222	        .mst_w_chan_t     ( w_chan_t            ),
223	        .mst_b_chan_t     ( mst_b_chan_t        ),
224	        .mst_ar_chan_t    ( mst_ar_chan_t       ),
225	        .mst_r_chan_t     ( mst_r_chan_t        )
226	      ) i_id_prepend (
227	        .pre_id_i         ( switch_id_t'(i)         ),
228	        .slv_aw_chans_i   ( slv_reqs_i[i].aw        ),
229	        .slv_aw_valids_i  ( slv_reqs_i[i].aw_valid  ),
230	        .slv_aw_readies_o ( slv_resps_o[i].aw_ready ),
231	        .slv_w_chans_i    ( slv_reqs_i[i].w         ),
232	        .slv_w_valids_i   ( slv_reqs_i[i].w_valid   ),
233	        .slv_w_readies_o  ( slv_resps_o[i].w_ready  ),
234	        .slv_b_chans_o    ( slv_resps_o[i].b        ),
235	        .slv_b_valids_o   ( slv_resps_o[i].b_valid  ),
236	        .slv_b_readies_i  ( slv_reqs_i[i].b_ready   ),
237	        .slv_ar_chans_i   ( slv_reqs_i[i].ar        ),
238	        .slv_ar_valids_i  ( slv_reqs_i[i].ar_valid  ),
239	        .slv_ar_readies_o ( slv_resps_o[i].ar_ready ),
240	        .slv_r_chans_o    ( slv_resps_o[i].r        ),
241	        .slv_r_valids_o   ( slv_resps_o[i].r_valid  ),
242	        .slv_r_readies_i  ( slv_reqs_i[i].r_ready   ),
243	        .mst_aw_chans_o   ( slv_aw_chans[i]         ),
244	        .mst_aw_valids_o  ( slv_aw_valids[i]        ),
245	        .mst_aw_readies_i ( slv_aw_readies[i]       ),
246	        .mst_w_chans_o    ( slv_w_chans[i]          ),
247	        .mst_w_valids_o   ( slv_w_valids[i]         ),
248	        .mst_w_readies_i  ( slv_w_readies[i]        ),
249	        .mst_b_chans_i    ( slv_b_chans[i]          ),
250	        .mst_b_valids_i   ( slv_b_valids[i]         ),
251	        .mst_b_readies_o  ( slv_b_readies[i]        ),
252	        .mst_ar_chans_o   ( slv_ar_chans[i]         ),
253	        .mst_ar_valids_o  ( slv_ar_valids[i]        ),
254	        .mst_ar_readies_i ( slv_ar_readies[i]       ),
255	        .mst_r_chans_i    ( slv_r_chans[i]          ),
256	        .mst_r_valids_i   ( slv_r_valids[i]         ),
257	        .mst_r_readies_o  ( slv_r_readies[i]        )
258	      );
259	    end
260	
261	    //--------------------------------------
262	    // AW Channel
263	    //--------------------------------------
264	    rr_arb_tree #(
265	      .NumIn    ( NoSlvPorts    ),
266	      .DataType ( mst_aw_chan_t ),
267	      .AxiVldRdy( 1'b1          ),
268	      .LockIn   ( 1'b1          )
269	    ) i_aw_arbiter (
270	      .clk_i  ( clk_i           ),
271	      .rst_ni ( rst_ni          ),
272	      .flush_i( 1'b0            ),
273	      .rr_i   ( '0              ),
274	      .req_i  ( slv_aw_valids   ),
275	      .gnt_o  ( slv_aw_readies  ),
276	      .data_i ( slv_aw_chans    ),
277	      .gnt_i  ( aw_ready        ),
278	      .req_o  ( aw_valid        ),
279	      .data_o ( mst_aw_chan     ),
280	      .idx_o  (                 )
281	    );
282	
283	    // control of the AW channel
284	    always_comb begin
285	      // default assignments
286	      lock_aw_valid_d = lock_aw_valid_q;
287	      load_aw_lock    = 1'b0;
288	      w_fifo_push     = 1'b0;
289	      mst_aw_valid    = 1'b0;
290	      aw_ready        = 1'b0;
291	      // had a downstream stall, be valid and send the AW along
292	      if (lock_aw_valid_q) begin
293	        mst_aw_valid = 1'b1;
294	        // transaction
295	        if (mst_aw_ready) begin
296	          aw_ready        = 1'b1;
297	          lock_aw_valid_d = 1'b0;
298	          load_aw_lock    = 1'b1;
299	        end
300	      end else begin
301	        if (!w_fifo_full && aw_valid) begin
302	          mst_aw_valid = 1'b1;
303	          w_fifo_push = 1'b1;
304	          if (mst_aw_ready) begin
305	            aw_ready = 1'b1;
306	          end else begin
307	            // go to lock if transaction not in this cycle
308	            lock_aw_valid_d = 1'b1;
309	            load_aw_lock    = 1'b1;
310	          end
311	        end
312	      end
313	    end
314	
315	    `FFLARN(lock_aw_valid_q, lock_aw_valid_d, load_aw_lock, '0, clk_i, rst_ni)
316	
317	    fifo_v3 #(
318	      .FALL_THROUGH ( FallThrough ),
319	      .DEPTH        ( MaxWTrans   ),
320	      .dtype        ( switch_id_t )
321	    ) i_w_fifo (
322	      .clk_i     ( clk_i                                     ),
323	      .rst_ni    ( rst_ni                                    ),
324	      .flush_i   ( 1'b0                                      ),
325	      .testmode_i( test_i                                    ),
326	      .full_o    ( w_fifo_full                               ),
327	      .empty_o   ( w_fifo_empty                              ),
328	      .usage_o   (                                           ),
329	      .data_i    ( mst_aw_chan.id[SlvAxiIDWidth+:MstIdxBits] ),
330	      .push_i    ( w_fifo_push                               ),
331	      .data_o    ( w_fifo_data                               ),
332	      .pop_i     ( w_fifo_pop                                )
333	    );
334	
335	    spill_register #(
336	      .T       ( mst_aw_chan_t ),
337	      .Bypass  ( ~SpillAw      ) // Param indicated that we want a spill reg
338	    ) i_aw_spill_reg (
339	      .clk_i   ( clk_i               ),
340	      .rst_ni  ( rst_ni              ),
341	      .valid_i ( mst_aw_valid        ),
342	      .ready_o ( mst_aw_ready        ),
343	      .data_i  ( mst_aw_chan         ),
344	      .valid_o ( mst_req_o.aw_valid  ),
345	      .ready_i ( mst_resp_i.aw_ready ),
346	      .data_o  ( mst_req_o.aw        )
347	    );
348	
349	    //--------------------------------------
350	    // W Channel
351	    //--------------------------------------
352	    // multiplexer
353	    assign mst_w_chan = slv_w_chans[w_fifo_data];
354	    always_comb begin
355	      // default assignments
356	      mst_w_valid   = 1'b0;
357	      slv_w_readies = '0;
358	      w_fifo_pop    = 1'b0;
359	      // control
360	      if (!w_fifo_empty) begin
361	        // connect the handshake
362	        mst_w_valid                = slv_w_valids[w_fifo_data];
363	        slv_w_readies[w_fifo_data] = mst_w_ready;
364	        // pop FIFO on a last transaction
365	        w_fifo_pop = slv_w_valids[w_fifo_data] & mst_w_ready & mst_w_chan.last;
366	      end
367	    end
368	
369	    spill_register #(
370	      .T       ( w_chan_t ),
371	      .Bypass  ( ~SpillW  )
372	    ) i_w_spill_reg (
373	      .clk_i   ( clk_i              ),
374	      .rst_ni  ( rst_ni             ),
375	      .valid_i ( mst_w_valid        ),
376	      .ready_o ( mst_w_ready        ),
377	      .data_i  ( mst_w_chan         ),
378	      .valid_o ( mst_req_o.w_valid  ),
379	      .ready_i ( mst_resp_i.w_ready ),
380	      .data_o  ( mst_req_o.w        )
381	    );
382	
383	    //--------------------------------------
384	    // B Channel
385	    //--------------------------------------
386	    // replicate B channels
387	    assign slv_b_chans  = {NoSlvPorts{mst_b_chan}};
388	    // control B channel handshake
389	    assign switch_b_id  = mst_b_chan.id[SlvAxiIDWidth+:MstIdxBits];
390	    assign slv_b_valids = (mst_b_valid) ? (1 << switch_b_id) : '0;
391	
392	    spill_register #(
393	      .T       ( mst_b_chan_t ),
394	      .Bypass  ( ~SpillB      )
395	    ) i_b_spill_reg (
396	      .clk_i   ( clk_i                      ),
397	      .rst_ni  ( rst_ni                     ),
398	      .valid_i ( mst_resp_i.b_valid         ),
399	      .ready_o ( mst_req_o.b_ready          ),
400	      .data_i  ( mst_resp_i.b               ),
401	      .valid_o ( mst_b_valid                ),
402	      .ready_i ( slv_b_readies[switch_b_id] ),
403	      .data_o  ( mst_b_chan                 )
404	    );
405	
406	    //--------------------------------------
407	    // AR Channel
408	    //--------------------------------------
409	    rr_arb_tree #(
410	      .NumIn    ( NoSlvPorts    ),
411	      .DataType ( mst_ar_chan_t ),
412	      .AxiVldRdy( 1'b1          ),
413	      .LockIn   ( 1'b1          )
414	    ) i_ar_arbiter (
415	      .clk_i  ( clk_i           ),
416	      .rst_ni ( rst_ni          ),
417	      .flush_i( 1'b0            ),
418	      .rr_i   ( '0              ),
419	      .req_i  ( slv_ar_valids   ),
420	      .gnt_o  ( slv_ar_readies  ),
421	      .data_i ( slv_ar_chans    ),
422	      .gnt_i  ( ar_ready        ),
423	      .req_o  ( ar_valid        ),
424	      .data_o ( mst_ar_chan     ),
425	      .idx_o  (                 )
426	    );
427	
428	    spill_register #(
429	      .T       ( mst_ar_chan_t ),
430	      .Bypass  ( ~SpillAr      )
431	    ) i_ar_spill_reg (
432	      .clk_i   ( clk_i               ),
433	      .rst_ni  ( rst_ni              ),
434	      .valid_i ( ar_valid            ),
435	      .ready_o ( ar_ready            ),
436	      .data_i  ( mst_ar_chan         ),
437	      .valid_o ( mst_req_o.ar_valid  ),
438	      .ready_i ( mst_resp_i.ar_ready ),
439	      .data_o  ( mst_req_o.ar        )
440	    );
441	
442	    //--------------------------------------
443	    // R Channel
444	    //--------------------------------------
445	    // replicate R channels
446	    assign slv_r_chans  = {NoSlvPorts{mst_r_chan}};
447	    // R channel handshake control
448	    assign switch_r_id  = mst_r_chan.id[SlvAxiIDWidth+:MstIdxBits];
449	    assign slv_r_valids = (mst_r_valid) ? (1 << switch_r_id) : '0;
450	
451	    spill_register #(
452	      .T       ( mst_r_chan_t ),
453	      .Bypass  ( ~SpillR      )
454	    ) i_r_spill_reg (
455	      .clk_i   ( clk_i                      ),
456	      .rst_ni  ( rst_ni                     ),
457	      .valid_i ( mst_resp_i.r_valid         ),
458	      .ready_o ( mst_req_o.r_ready          ),
459	      .data_i  ( mst_resp_i.r               ),
460	      .valid_o ( mst_r_valid                ),
461	      .ready_i ( slv_r_readies[switch_r_id] ),
462	      .data_o  ( mst_r_chan                 )
463	    );
464	  end
465	
466	// pragma translate_off
467	`ifndef VERILATOR
468	  initial begin
469	    assert (SlvAxiIDWidth > 0) else $fatal(1, "AXI ID width of slave ports must be non-zero!");
470	    assert (NoSlvPorts > 0) else $fatal(1, "Number of slave ports must be non-zero!");
471	    assert (MaxWTrans > 0)
472	      else $fatal(1, "Maximum number of outstanding writes must be non-zero!");
473	    assert (MstAxiIDWidth >= SlvAxiIDWidth + $clog2(NoSlvPorts))
474	      else $fatal(1, "AXI ID width of master ports must be wide enough to identify slave ports!");
475	    // Assert ID widths (one slave is sufficient since they all have the same type).
476	    assert ($unsigned($bits(slv_reqs_i[0].aw.id)) == SlvAxiIDWidth)
477	      else $fatal(1, "ID width of AW channel of slave ports does not match parameter!");
478	    assert ($unsigned($bits(slv_reqs_i[0].ar.id)) == SlvAxiIDWidth)
479	      else $fatal(1, "ID width of AR channel of slave ports does not match parameter!");
480	    assert ($unsigned($bits(slv_resps_o[0].b.id)) == SlvAxiIDWidth)
481	      else $fatal(1, "ID width of B channel of slave ports does not match parameter!");
482	    assert ($unsigned($bits(slv_resps_o[0].r.id)) == SlvAxiIDWidth)
483	      else $fatal(1, "ID width of R channel of slave ports does not match parameter!");
484	    assert ($unsigned($bits(mst_req_o.aw.id)) == MstAxiIDWidth)
485	      else $fatal(1, "ID width of AW channel of master port is wrong!");
486	    assert ($unsigned($bits(mst_req_o.ar.id)) == MstAxiIDWidth)
487	      else $fatal(1, "ID width of AR channel of master port is wrong!");
488	    assert ($unsigned($bits(mst_resp_i.b.id)) == MstAxiIDWidth)
489	      else $fatal(1, "ID width of B channel of master port is wrong!");
490	    assert ($unsigned($bits(mst_resp_i.r.id)) == MstAxiIDWidth)
491	      else $fatal(1, "ID width of R channel of master port is wrong!");
492	  end
493	`endif
494	// pragma translate_on
495	endmodule

498	`include "axi/assign.svh"
499	`include "axi/typedef.svh"
500	module axi_mux_intf #(
501	  parameter int unsigned SLV_AXI_ID_WIDTH = 32'd0, // Synopsys DC requires default value for params
502	  parameter int unsigned MST_AXI_ID_WIDTH = 32'd0,
503	  parameter int unsigned AXI_ADDR_WIDTH   = 32'd0,
504	  parameter int unsigned AXI_DATA_WIDTH   = 32'd0,
505	  parameter int unsigned AXI_USER_WIDTH   = 32'd0,
506	  parameter int unsigned NO_SLV_PORTS     = 32'd0, // Number of slave ports
507	  // Maximum number of outstanding transactions per write
508	  parameter int unsigned MAX_W_TRANS      = 32'd8,
509	  // if enabled, this multiplexer is purely combinatorial
510	  parameter bit          FALL_THROUGH     = 1'b0,
511	  // add spill register on write master ports, adds a cycle latency on write channels
512	  parameter bit          SPILL_AW         = 1'b1,
513	  parameter bit          SPILL_W          = 1'b0,
514	  parameter bit          SPILL_B          = 1'b0,
515	  // add spill register on read master ports, adds a cycle latency on read channels
516	  parameter bit          SPILL_AR         = 1'b1,
517	  parameter bit          SPILL_R          = 1'b0
518	) (
519	  input  logic   clk_i,                  // Clock
520	  input  logic   rst_ni,                 // Asynchronous reset active low
521	  input  logic   test_i,                 // Testmode enable
522	  AXI_BUS.Slave  slv [NO_SLV_PORTS-1:0], // slave ports
523	  AXI_BUS.Master mst                     // master port
524	);
525	
526	  typedef logic [SLV_AXI_ID_WIDTH-1:0] slv_id_t;
527	  typedef logic [MST_AXI_ID_WIDTH-1:0] mst_id_t;
528	  typedef logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH -1:0]  addr_t;
529	  typedef logic [AXI_DATA_WIDTH-1:0]   data_t;
530	  typedef logic [AXI_DATA_WIDTH/8-1:0] strb_t;
531	  typedef logic [AXI_USER_WIDTH-1:0]   user_t;
532	  // channels typedef
533	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_AW_CHAN_T(slv_aw_chan_t, addr_t, slv_id_t, user_t)

539	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_B_CHAN_T(mst_b_chan_t, mst_id_t, user_t)
540	
541	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_AR_CHAN_T(slv_ar_chan_t, addr_t, slv_id_t, user_t)
542	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_AR_CHAN_T(mst_ar_chan_t, addr_t, mst_id_t, user_t)
543	
544	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_R_CHAN_T(slv_r_chan_t, data_t, slv_id_t, user_t)
545	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_R_CHAN_T(mst_r_chan_t, data_t, mst_id_t, user_t)
546	
547	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_REQ_T(slv_req_t, slv_aw_chan_t, w_chan_t, slv_ar_chan_t)
548	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_RESP_T(slv_resp_t, slv_b_chan_t, slv_r_chan_t)
549	
550	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_REQ_T(mst_req_t, mst_aw_chan_t, w_chan_t, mst_ar_chan_t)
551	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_RESP_T(mst_resp_t, mst_b_chan_t, mst_r_chan_t)
552	
553	  slv_req_t  [NO_SLV_PORTS-1:0] slv_reqs;
554	  slv_resp_t [NO_SLV_PORTS-1:0] slv_resps;
555	  mst_req_t                     mst_req;
556	  mst_resp_t                    mst_resp;
557	
558	  for (genvar i = 0; i < NO_SLV_PORTS; i++) begin : gen_assign_slv_ports
559	    `AXI_ASSIGN_TO_REQ(slv_reqs[i], slv[i])
560	    `AXI_ASSIGN_FROM_RESP(slv[i], slv_resps[i])
561	  end
562	
563	  `AXI_ASSIGN_FROM_REQ(mst, mst_req)
564	  `AXI_ASSIGN_TO_RESP(mst_resp, mst)
565	
566	  axi_mux #(
567	    .SlvAxiIDWidth ( SLV_AXI_ID_WIDTH ),
568	    .slv_aw_chan_t ( slv_aw_chan_t    ), // AW Channel Type, slave ports
569	    .mst_aw_chan_t ( mst_aw_chan_t    ), // AW Channel Type, master port
570	    .w_chan_t      ( w_chan_t         ), //  W Channel Type, all ports
571	    .slv_b_chan_t  ( slv_b_chan_t     ), //  B Channel Type, slave ports
572	    .mst_b_chan_t  ( mst_b_chan_t     ), //  B Channel Type, master port
573	    .slv_ar_chan_t ( slv_ar_chan_t    ), // AR Channel Type, slave ports
574	    .mst_ar_chan_t ( mst_ar_chan_t    ), // AR Channel Type, master port
575	    .slv_r_chan_t  ( slv_r_chan_t     ), //  R Channel Type, slave ports
576	    .mst_r_chan_t  ( mst_r_chan_t     ), //  R Channel Type, master port

579	    .mst_req_t     ( mst_req_t        ),
580	    .mst_resp_t    ( mst_resp_t       ),

582	    .MaxWTrans     ( MAX_W_TRANS      ),
583	    .FallThrough   ( FALL_THROUGH     ),
584	    .SpillAw       ( SPILL_AW         ),

27	/// Demultiplex one AXI4+ATOP slave port to multiple AXI4+ATOP master ports.
28	///
29	/// The AW and AR slave channels each have a `select` input to determine to which master port the
30	/// current request is sent.  The `select` can, for example, be driven by an address decoding module
31	/// to map address ranges to different AXI slaves.
32	///
33	/// ## Design overview
34	///
35	/// ![Block diagram](module.axi_demux.png "Block diagram")
36	///
37	/// Beats on the W channel are routed by demultiplexer according to the selection for the
38	/// corresponding AW beat.  This relies on the AXI property that W bursts must be sent in the same
39	/// order as AW beats and beats from different W bursts may not be interleaved.
40	///
41	/// Beats on the B and R channel are multiplexed from the master ports to the slave port with
42	/// a round-robin arbitration tree.
43	module axi_demux_simple #(
44	  parameter int unsigned AxiIdWidth     = 32'd0,
45	  parameter bit          AtopSupport    = 1'b1,
46	  parameter type         axi_req_t      = logic,
47	  parameter type         axi_resp_t     = logic,
48	  parameter int unsigned NoMstPorts     = 32'd0,
49	  parameter int unsigned MaxTrans       = 32'd8,
50	  parameter int unsigned AxiLookBits    = 32'd3,
51	  parameter bit          UniqueIds      = 1'b0,
52	  // Dependent parameters, DO NOT OVERRIDE!
53	  parameter int unsigned SelectWidth    = (NoMstPorts > 32'd1) ? $clog2(NoMstPorts) : 32'd1,
54	  parameter type         select_t       = logic [SelectWidth-1:0]
55	) (
56	  input  logic                          clk_i,
57	  input  logic                          rst_ni,
58	  input  logic                          test_i,
59	  // Slave Port
60	  input  axi_req_t                      slv_req_i,
61	  input  select_t                       slv_aw_select_i,
62	  input  select_t                       slv_ar_select_i,
63	  output axi_resp_t                     slv_resp_o,
64	  // Master Ports
65	  output axi_req_t    [NoMstPorts-1:0]  mst_reqs_o,
66	  input  axi_resp_t   [NoMstPorts-1:0]  mst_resps_i
67	);
68	
69	  localparam int unsigned IdCounterWidth = cf_math_pkg::idx_width(MaxTrans);
70	  typedef logic [IdCounterWidth-1:0] id_cnt_t;
71	
72	  // pass through if only one master port
73	  if (NoMstPorts == 32'h1) begin : gen_no_demux
74	    `AXI_ASSIGN_REQ_STRUCT(mst_reqs_o[0], slv_req_i)
75	    `AXI_ASSIGN_RESP_STRUCT(slv_resp_o, mst_resps_i[0])
76	  end else begin
77	
78	    //--------------------------------------
79	    //--------------------------------------
80	    // Signal Declarations
81	    //--------------------------------------
82	    //--------------------------------------
83	
84	    //--------------------------------------
85	    // Write Transaction
86	    //--------------------------------------
87	
88	    // Register which locks the AW valid signal
89	    logic                     lock_aw_valid_d,    lock_aw_valid_q, load_aw_lock;
90	    logic                     aw_valid,           aw_ready;
91	
92	    // AW ID counter
93	    select_t                  lookup_aw_select;
94	    logic                     aw_select_occupied, aw_id_cnt_full;
95	    // Upon an ATOP load, inject IDs from the AW into the AR channel
96	    logic                     atop_inject;
97	
98	    // W select counter: stores the decision to which master W beats should go
99	    select_t                  w_select,           w_select_q;
100	    logic                     w_select_valid;
101	    id_cnt_t                  w_open;
102	    logic                     w_cnt_up,           w_cnt_down;
103	
104	    // B channles input into the arbitration
105	    logic    [NoMstPorts-1:0] mst_b_valids,       mst_b_readies;
106	
107	    //--------------------------------------
108	    // Read Transaction
109	    //--------------------------------------
110	
111	    // AR ID counter
112	    select_t                  lookup_ar_select;
113	    logic                     ar_select_occupied, ar_id_cnt_full;
114	    logic                     ar_push;
115	
116	    // Register which locks the AR valid signel
117	    logic                     lock_ar_valid_d,    lock_ar_valid_q, load_ar_lock;
118	    logic                     ar_valid,           ar_ready;
119	
120	    logic    [NoMstPorts-1:0] mst_r_valids, mst_r_readies;
121	
122

126	
127	
128	    //--------------------------------------
129	    // Channel Control
130	    //--------------------------------------
131	    //--------------------------------------
132	
133	    //--------------------------------------
134	    // AW Channel
135	    //--------------------------------------
136	
137	    // Control of the AW handshake
138	    always_comb begin
139	      // AXI Handshakes
140	      slv_resp_o.aw_ready = 1'b0;
141	      aw_valid     = 1'b0;
142	      // `lock_aw_valid`, used to be protocol conform as it is not allowed to deassert
143	      // a valid if there was no corresponding ready. As this process has to be able to inject
144	      // an AXI ID into the counter of the AR channel on an ATOP, there could be a case where
145	      // this process waits on `aw_ready` but in the mean time on the AR channel the counter gets
146	      // full.
147	      lock_aw_valid_d = lock_aw_valid_q;
148	      load_aw_lock    = 1'b0;
149	      // AW ID counter and W FIFO
150	      w_cnt_up        = 1'b0;
151	      // ATOP injection into ar counter
152	      atop_inject     = 1'b0;
153	      // we had an arbitration decision, the valid is locked, wait for the transaction
154	      if (lock_aw_valid_q) begin
155	        aw_valid = 1'b1;
156	        // transaction
157	        if (aw_ready) begin
158	          slv_resp_o.aw_ready    = 1'b1;
159	          lock_aw_valid_d = 1'b0;
160	          load_aw_lock    = 1'b1;
161	          // inject the ATOP if necessary
162	          atop_inject     = slv_req_i.aw.atop[axi_pkg::ATOP_R_RESP] & AtopSupport;
163	        end
164	      end else begin
165	        // An AW can be handled if `i_aw_id_counter` and `i_counter_open_w` are not full.  An ATOP that
166	        // requires an R response can be handled if additionally `i_ar_id_counter` is not full (this
167	        // only applies if ATOPs are supported at all).
168	        if (!aw_id_cnt_full && (w_open != {IdCounterWidth{1'b1}}) &&
169	            (!(ar_id_cnt_full && slv_req_i.aw.atop[axi_pkg::ATOP_R_RESP]) ||
170	             !AtopSupport)) begin
171	          // There is a valid AW vector make the id lookup and go further, if it passes.
172	          // Also stall if previous transmitted AWs still have active W's in flight.
173	          // This prevents deadlocking of the W channel. The counters are there for the
174	          // Handling of the B responses.
175	          if (slv_req_i.aw_valid &&
176	                ((w_open == '0) || (w_select == slv_aw_select_i)) &&
177	                (!aw_select_occupied || (slv_aw_select_i == lookup_aw_select))) begin
178	            // connect the handshake
179	            aw_valid     = 1'b1;
180	            // push arbitration to the W FIFO regardless, do not wait for the AW transaction
181	            w_cnt_up     = 1'b1;
182	            // on AW transaction
183	            if (aw_ready) begin
184	              slv_resp_o.aw_ready = 1'b1;
185	              atop_inject  = slv_req_i.aw.atop[axi_pkg::ATOP_R_RESP] & AtopSupport;
186	            // no AW transaction this cycle, lock the decision
187	            end else begin
188	              lock_aw_valid_d = 1'b1;
189	              load_aw_lock    = 1'b1;
190	            end
191	          end
192	        end
193	      end
194	    end
195	
196	    // lock the valid signal, as the selection gets pushed into the W FIFO on first assertion,
197	    // prevent further pushing
198	    `FFLARN(lock_aw_valid_q, lock_aw_valid_d, load_aw_lock, '0, clk_i, rst_ni)
199	
200	    if (UniqueIds) begin : gen_unique_ids_aw
201	      // If the `UniqueIds` parameter is set, each write transaction has an ID that is unique among
202	      // all in-flight write transactions, or all write transactions with a given ID target the same
203	      // master port as all write transactions with the same ID, or both.  This means that the
204	      // signals that are driven by the ID counters if this parameter is not set can instead be
205	      // derived from existing signals.  The ID counters can therefore be omitted.
206	      assign lookup_aw_select = slv_aw_select_i;
207	      assign aw_select_occupied = 1'b0;
208	      assign aw_id_cnt_full = 1'b0;
209	    end else begin : gen_aw_id_counter
210	      axi_demux_id_counters #(
211	        .AxiIdBits         ( AxiLookBits    ),
212	        .CounterWidth      ( IdCounterWidth ),
213	        .mst_port_select_t ( select_t       )
214	      ) i_aw_id_counter (
215	        .clk_i                        ( clk_i                          ),
216	        .rst_ni                       ( rst_ni                         ),
217	        .lookup_axi_id_i              ( slv_req_i.aw.id[0+:AxiLookBits] ),
218	        .lookup_mst_select_o          ( lookup_aw_select               ),
219	        .lookup_mst_select_occupied_o ( aw_select_occupied             ),
220	        .full_o                       ( aw_id_cnt_full                 ),
221	        .inject_axi_id_i              ( '0                             ),
222	        .inject_i                     ( 1'b0                           ),
223	        .push_axi_id_i                ( slv_req_i.aw.id[0+:AxiLookBits] ),
224	        .push_mst_select_i            ( slv_aw_select_i                ),
225	        .push_i                       ( w_cnt_up                       ),
226	        .pop_axi_id_i                 ( slv_resp_o.b.id[0+:AxiLookBits]  ),
227	        .pop_i                        ( slv_resp_o.b_valid & slv_req_i.b_ready      ),
228	        .any_outstanding_trx_o        ( )
229	      );
230	      // pop from ID counter on outward transaction
231	    end
232	
233	    // This counter steers the demultiplexer of the W channel.
234	    // `w_select` determines, which handshaking is connected.
235	    // AWs are only forwarded, if the counter is empty, or `w_select_q` is the same as
236	    // `slv_aw_select_i`.
237	    counter #(
238	      .WIDTH           ( IdCounterWidth ),
239	      .STICKY_OVERFLOW ( 1'b0           )
240	    ) i_counter_open_w (
241	      .clk_i,
242	      .rst_ni,
243	      .clear_i    ( 1'b0                  ),
244	      .en_i       ( w_cnt_up ^ w_cnt_down ),
245	      .load_i     ( 1'b0                  ),
246	      .down_i     ( w_cnt_down            ),
247	      .d_i        ( '0                    ),
248	      .q_o        ( w_open                ),
249	      .overflow_o ( /*not used*/          )
250	    );
251	
252	    `FFLARN(w_select_q, slv_aw_select_i, w_cnt_up, select_t'(0), clk_i, rst_ni)
253	    assign w_select       = (|w_open) ? w_select_q : slv_aw_select_i;
254	    assign w_select_valid = w_cnt_up | (|w_open);
255	
256	    //--------------------------------------
257	    //  W Channel
258	    //--------------------------------------
259	
260	    //--------------------------------------
261	    //  B Channel
262	    //--------------------------------------
263	    logic [cf_math_pkg::idx_width(NoMstPorts)-1:0] b_idx;
264	
265	    // Arbitration of the different B responses
266	    rr_arb_tree #(
267	      .NumIn    ( NoMstPorts ),
268	      .DataType ( logic   ),
269	      .AxiVldRdy( 1'b1       ),
270	      .LockIn   ( 1'b1       )
271	    ) i_b_mux (
272	      .clk_i  ( clk_i         ),
273	      .rst_ni ( rst_ni        ),
274	      .flush_i( 1'b0          ),
275	      .rr_i   ( '0            ),
276	      .req_i  ( mst_b_valids  ),
277	      .gnt_o  ( mst_b_readies ),
278	      .data_i ( '0   ),
279	      .gnt_i  ( slv_req_i.b_ready  ),
280	      .req_o  ( slv_resp_o.b_valid ),
281	      .data_o (        ),
282	      .idx_o  ( b_idx              )
283	    );
284	
285	    always_comb begin
286	      if (slv_resp_o.b_valid) begin
287	        `AXI_SET_B_STRUCT(slv_resp_o.b, mst_resps_i[b_idx].b)
288	      end else begin
289	        slv_resp_o.b = '0;
290	      end
291	    end
292	
293	    //--------------------------------------
294	    //  AR Channel
295	    //--------------------------------------
296	
297	    // control of the AR handshake
298	    always_comb begin
299	      // AXI Handshakes
300	      slv_resp_o.ar_ready    = 1'b0;
301	      ar_valid        = 1'b0;
302	      // `lock_ar_valid`: Used to be protocol conform as it is not allowed to deassert `ar_valid`
303	      // if there was no corresponding `ar_ready`. There is the possibility that an injection
304	      // of a R response from an `atop` from the AW channel can change the occupied flag of the
305	      // `i_ar_id_counter`, even if it was previously empty. This FF prevents the deassertion.
306	      lock_ar_valid_d = lock_ar_valid_q;
307	      load_ar_lock    = 1'b0;
308	      // AR id counter
309	      ar_push         = 1'b0;
310	      // The process had an arbitration decision in a previous cycle, the valid is locked,
311	      // wait for the AR transaction.
312	      if (lock_ar_valid_q) begin
313	        ar_valid = 1'b1;
314	        // transaction
315	        if (ar_ready) begin
316	          slv_resp_o.ar_ready    = 1'b1;
317	          ar_push         = 1'b1;
318	          lock_ar_valid_d = 1'b0;
319	          load_ar_lock    = 1'b1;
320	        end
321	      end else begin
322	        // The process can start handling AR transaction if `i_ar_id_counter` has space.
323	        if (!ar_id_cnt_full) begin
324	          // There is a valid AR, so look the ID up.
325	          if (slv_req_i.ar_valid && (!ar_select_occupied ||
326	             (slv_ar_select_i == lookup_ar_select))) begin
327	            // connect the AR handshake
328	            ar_valid     = 1'b1;
329	            // on transaction
330	            if (ar_ready) begin
331	              slv_resp_o.ar_ready = 1'b1;
332	              ar_push      = 1'b1;
333	            // no transaction this cycle, lock the valid decision!
334	            end else begin
335	              lock_ar_valid_d = 1'b1;
336	              load_ar_lock    = 1'b1;
337	            end
338	          end
339	        end
340	      end
341	    end
342	
343	    // this ff is needed so that ar does not get de-asserted if an atop gets injected
344	    `FFLARN(lock_ar_valid_q, lock_ar_valid_d, load_ar_lock, '0, clk_i, rst_ni)
345	
346	    if (UniqueIds) begin : gen_unique_ids_ar
347	      // If the `UniqueIds` parameter is set, each read transaction has an ID that is unique among
348	      // all in-flight read transactions, or all read transactions with a given ID target the same
349	      // master port as all read transactions with the same ID, or both.  This means that the
350	      // signals that are driven by the ID counters if this parameter is not set can instead be
351	      // derived from existing signals.  The ID counters can therefore be omitted.
352	      assign lookup_ar_select = slv_ar_select_i;
353	      assign ar_select_occupied = 1'b0;
354	      assign ar_id_cnt_full = 1'b0;
355	    end else begin : gen_ar_id_counter
356	      axi_demux_id_counters #(
357	        .AxiIdBits         ( AxiLookBits    ),
358	        .CounterWidth      ( IdCounterWidth ),
359	        .mst_port_select_t ( select_t       )
360	      ) i_ar_id_counter (
361	        .clk_i                        ( clk_i                                       ),
362	        .rst_ni                       ( rst_ni                                      ),
363	        .lookup_axi_id_i              ( slv_req_i.ar.id[0+:AxiLookBits]             ),
364	        .lookup_mst_select_o          ( lookup_ar_select                            ),
365	        .lookup_mst_select_occupied_o ( ar_select_occupied                          ),
366	        .full_o                       ( ar_id_cnt_full                              ),
367	        .inject_axi_id_i              ( slv_req_i.aw.id[0+:AxiLookBits]             ),
368	        .inject_i                     ( atop_inject                                 ),
369	        .push_axi_id_i                ( slv_req_i.ar.id[0+:AxiLookBits]             ),
370	        .push_mst_select_i            ( slv_ar_select_i                             ),
371	        .push_i                       ( ar_push                                     ),
372	        .pop_axi_id_i                 ( slv_resp_o.r.id[0+:AxiLookBits]               ),
373	        .pop_i                        ( slv_resp_o.r_valid & slv_req_i.r_ready & slv_resp_o.r.last ),
374	        .any_outstanding_trx_o        ( )
375	      );
376	    end
377	
378	    //--------------------------------------
379	    //  R Channel
380	    //--------------------------------------
381	
382	    logic [cf_math_pkg::idx_width(NoMstPorts)-1:0] r_idx;
383	
384	    // Arbitration of the different r responses
385	    rr_arb_tree #(
386	      .NumIn    ( NoMstPorts ),
387	      .DataType ( logic   ),
388	      .AxiVldRdy( 1'b1       ),
389	      .LockIn   ( 1'b1       )
390	    ) i_r_mux (
391	      .clk_i  ( clk_i         ),
392	      .rst_ni ( rst_ni        ),
393	      .flush_i( 1'b0          ),
394	      .rr_i   ( '0            ),
395	      .req_i  ( mst_r_valids  ),
396	      .gnt_o  ( mst_r_readies ),
397	      .data_i ( '0            ),
398	      .gnt_i  ( slv_req_i.r_ready   ),
399	      .req_o  ( slv_resp_o.r_valid   ),
400	      .data_o (),
401	      .idx_o  ( r_idx              )
402	    );
403	
404	    always_comb begin
405	      if (slv_resp_o.r_valid) begin
406	        `AXI_SET_R_STRUCT(slv_resp_o.r, mst_resps_i[r_idx].r)
407	      end else begin
408	        slv_resp_o.r = '0;
409	      end
410	    end
411	
412	    assign ar_ready = ar_valid & mst_resps_i[slv_ar_select_i].ar_ready;
413	    assign aw_ready = aw_valid & mst_resps_i[slv_aw_select_i].aw_ready;
414	
415	    // process that defines the individual demuxes and assignments for the arbitration
416	    // as mst_reqs_o has to be drivem from the same always comb block!
417	    always_comb begin
418	      // default assignments
419	      mst_reqs_o  = '0;
420	      slv_resp_o.w_ready = 1'b0;
421	      w_cnt_down  = 1'b0;
422	
423	      for (int unsigned i = 0; i < NoMstPorts; i++) begin
424	        // AW channel
425	        mst_reqs_o[i].aw       = slv_req_i.aw;
426	        mst_reqs_o[i].aw_valid = 1'b0;
427	        if (aw_valid && (slv_aw_select_i == i)) begin
428	          mst_reqs_o[i].aw_valid = 1'b1;
429	        end
430	
431	        //  W channel
432	        mst_reqs_o[i].w       = slv_req_i.w;
433	        mst_reqs_o[i].w_valid = 1'b0;
434	        if (w_select_valid && (w_select == i)) begin
435	          mst_reqs_o[i].w_valid = slv_req_i.w_valid;
436	          slv_resp_o.w_ready           = mst_resps_i[i].w_ready;
437	          w_cnt_down            = slv_req_i.w_valid & mst_resps_i[i].w_ready & slv_req_i.w.last;
438	        end
439	
440	        //  B channel
441	        mst_reqs_o[i].b_ready = mst_b_readies[i];
442	
443	        // AR channel
444	        mst_reqs_o[i].ar       = slv_req_i.ar;
445	        mst_reqs_o[i].ar_valid = 1'b0;
446	        if (ar_valid && (slv_ar_select_i == i)) begin
447	          mst_reqs_o[i].ar_valid = 1'b1;
448	        end
449	
450	        //  R channel
451	        mst_reqs_o[i].r_ready = mst_r_readies[i];
452	      end
453	    end
454	    // unpack the response B and R channels for the arbitration
455	    for (genvar i = 0; i < NoMstPorts; i++) begin : gen_b_channels
456	      // assign mst_b_chans[i]        = mst_resps_i[i].b;
457	      assign mst_b_valids[i]       = mst_resps_i[i].b_valid;
458	      // assign mst_r_chans[i]        = mst_resps_i[i].r;
459	      assign mst_r_valids[i]       = mst_resps_i[i].r_valid;
460	    end
461	
462	// Validate parameters.
463	// pragma translate_off
464	`ifndef VERILATOR
465	    initial begin: validate_params
466	      no_mst_ports: assume (NoMstPorts > 0) else
467	        $fatal(1, "The Number of slaves (NoMstPorts) has to be at least 1");
468	      AXI_ID_BITS:  assume (AxiIdWidth >= AxiLookBits) else
469	        $fatal(1, "AxiIdBits has to be equal or smaller than AxiIdWidth.");
470	    end
471	`ifndef XSIM
472	    default disable iff (!rst_ni);
473	    aw_select: assume property( @(posedge clk_i) (slv_req_i.aw_valid |->
474	                                                 (slv_aw_select_i < NoMstPorts))) else
475	      $fatal(1, "slv_aw_select_i is %d: AW has selected a slave that is not defined.\
476	                 NoMstPorts: %d", slv_aw_select_i, NoMstPorts);
477	    ar_select: assume property( @(posedge clk_i) (slv_req_i.ar_valid |->
478	                                                 (slv_ar_select_i < NoMstPorts))) else
479	      $fatal(1, "slv_ar_select_i is %d: AR has selected a slave that is not defined.\
480	                 NoMstPorts: %d", slv_ar_select_i, NoMstPorts);
481	    aw_valid_stable: assert property( @(posedge clk_i) (aw_valid && !aw_ready) |=> aw_valid) else
482	      $fatal(1, "aw_valid was deasserted, when aw_ready = 0 in last cycle.");
483	    ar_valid_stable: assert property( @(posedge clk_i)
484	                               (ar_valid && !ar_ready) |=> ar_valid) else
485	      $fatal(1, "ar_valid was deasserted, when ar_ready = 0 in last cycle.");
486	    slv_aw_chan_stable: assert property( @(posedge clk_i) (aw_valid && !aw_ready)
487	                               |=> $stable(slv_req_i.aw)) else
488	      $fatal(1, "slv_aw_chan unstable with valid set.");
489	    slv_aw_select_stable: assert property( @(posedge clk_i) (aw_valid && !aw_ready)
490	                               |=> $stable(slv_aw_select_i)) else
491	      $fatal(1, "slv_aw_select_i unstable with valid set.");
492	    slv_ar_chan_stable: assert property( @(posedge clk_i) (ar_valid && !ar_ready)
493	                               |=> $stable(slv_req_i.ar)) else
494	      $fatal(1, "slv_ar_chan unstable with valid set.");
495	    slv_ar_select_stable: assert property( @(posedge clk_i) (ar_valid && !ar_ready)
496	                               |=> $stable(slv_ar_select_i)) else
497	      $fatal(1, "slv_ar_select_i unstable with valid set.");
498	    internal_ar_select: assert property( @(posedge clk_i)
499	        (ar_valid |-> slv_ar_select_i < NoMstPorts))
500	      else $fatal(1, "slv_ar_select_i illegal while ar_valid.");
501	    internal_aw_select: assert property( @(posedge clk_i)
502	        (aw_valid |-> slv_aw_select_i < NoMstPorts))
503	      else $fatal(1, "slv_aw_select_i illegal while aw_valid.");
504	    w_underflow: assert property( @(posedge clk_i)
505	        ((w_open == '0) && (w_cnt_up ^ w_cnt_down) |-> !w_cnt_down)) else
506	        $fatal(1, "W counter underflowed!");
507	    `ASSUME(NoAtopAllowed, !AtopSupport && slv_req_i.aw_valid |-> slv_req_i.aw.atop == '0)
508	`endif
509	`endif
510	// pragma translate_on
511	  end
512	endmodule
513

1	# Documentation of AXI Modules
2	
3	This folder contains the documentation of the following modules:
4	
5	- [AXI Crossbar (`axi_xbar`)](axi_xbar.md)
6	- [AXI Demultiplexer (`axi_demux`)](axi_demux.md)
7	- [AXI Multiplexer (`axi_mux`)](axi_mux.md)
8	- [AXI4-Lite Mailbox (`axi_lite_mailbox`)](axi_lite_mailbox.md)
9	
10	
11	## Relevant Specifications
12	
13	We adhere to the *AMBA AXI and ACE Protocol Specification, Issue F.b*, abbreviated as *AXI Spec* in these documents.
14	
15	
16	## Terminology
17	
18	We follow the terminology defined in the AXI Glossary (see p. 433 of the AXI Spec).
19	
20	Additionally, we define the following terms.
21	
22	### Handshake
23	
24	On a channel with valid and ready signals, a *handshake* occurs when both valid and ready are high on a clock edge.  See section A3.2.1 of the AXI Spec for details on the handshake process.
25	
26	### In Flight
27	
28	A transaction is *in flight* for a given interface when the handshake of the `Ax` beat of a transaction has occurred on that interface but the handshake of the (last) response of that transaction has not yet occurred on that interface.
29	
30	### Pending
31	
32	A handshake is *pending* for a given channel when the valid signal is high and the ready signal is low.

file.content:
# Documentation of AXI Modules

This folder contains the documentation of the following modules:

- [AXI Crossbar (`axi_xbar`)](axi_xbar.md)
- [AXI Demultiplexer (`axi_demux`)](axi_demux.md)
- [AXI Multiplexer (`axi_mux`)](axi_mux.md)
- [AXI4-Lite Mailbox (`axi_lite_mailbox`)](axi_lite_mailbox.md)


## Relevant Specifications

We adhere to the *AMBA AXI and ACE Protocol Specification, Issue F.b*, abbreviated as *AXI Spec* in these documents.


## Terminology

We follow the terminology defined in the AXI Glossary (see p. 433 of the AXI Spec).

Additionally, we define the following terms.

### Handshake

On a channel with valid and ready signals, a *handshake* occurs when both valid and ready are high on a clock edge.  See section A3.2.1 of the AXI Spec for details on the handshake process.

### In Flight

A transaction is *in flight* for a given interface when the handshake of the `Ax` beat of a transaction has occurred on that interface but the handshake of the (last) response of that transaction has not yet occurred on that interface.

### Pending

A handshake is *pending* for a given channel when the valid signal is high and the ready signal is low.

content.thinking:
Now I have all the source material I need. Let me note the key line numbers for citations:

**doc/axi_xbar.md:**
- Lines 82-87: "Ordering and Stalls" section (the stall behavior + no reorder buffer rationale)
- Line 51: AxiIdUsedSlvPorts config field
- Lines 94-109: "Design Rationale for No Pipelining Inside Crossbar"
- Lines 96-102: deadlock criteria
- Lines 103-109: detailed explanation
- Line 63: recommended config (CUT_ALL_AX, FallThrough=0)

**src/axi_mux.sv:**
- Lines 168-184: lock_aw_valid FF and W FIFO signals (this FF removes AW to W dependency)
- Lines 172-179: comments about locking AW valid
- Lines 264-281: rr_arb_tree AW arbiter with LockIn=1
- Lines 317-333: fifo_v3 i_w_fifo (the W FIFO)
- Lines 284-313: AW control with w_fifo_push, lock_aw_valid
- Lines 354-367: W channel control, w_fifo_pop on last
- Line 68: MstIdxBits = $clog2(NoSlvPorts)

**src/axi_demux.sv:**
- Lines 36-41: design overview comment (W routing relies on AXI property)
- Lines 89-187: spill registers
- Lines 189-209: axi_demux_simple instantiation

**src/axi_demux_simple.sv (supplementary, for the actual stall logic):**
- Lines 172-177: "stall if previous transmitted AWs still have active W's in flight. This prevents deadlocking of the W channel."
- Lines 175-177: AW handling gate with w_open and aw_select_occupied (the actual stall condition)

Now let me design the lecture. The topic is ordering, stalls, and deadlock-free design. The minimal modules are doc/axi_xbar, axi_demux, axi_mux.

For module 4.1 (ordering + stall):

Concept: AXI4 ordering model. Transactions with same ID in same direction must complete in order at the master (the component that issued them sees responses in the order it issued requests). This is the AXI "ordering model" / ordering guarantees.

When a slave port of the xbar/demux receives two transactions with same ID & direction but routed to two different master ports (downstream slaves), the responses could return out of order (because they go to physically different downstream slaves). Since the xbar has no reorder buffer, it cannot re-sort them. Therefore, the only safe thing is: don't let the second transaction enter flight until the first one completes. That means stall the AW (or AR) channel.

The mechanism: axi_demux_id_counters track per-ID in-flight count + which master port the in-flight txn targets. On a new AW/AR, lookup the ID; if it's already in-flight to a different master port, stall (don't assert ready).

Module 4.2 (AxiIdUsedSlvPorts):

Compare only lowest N bits. N = AxiIdUsedSlvPorts in xbar (= AxiLookBits in demux). Counters = 2^N per direction. 
- N = full width: no false conflicts (different full IDs never falsely match), but 2^AxiIdWidth counters — huge area.
- N small: cheap, but two transactions whose low N bits coincide but full IDs differ get falsely treated as "same ID" → unnecessary stall (false conflict / aliasing). Still correct (over-conservative), just throughput loss.
- Tradeoff: area/delay vs throughput.
- UniqueIds = 1: if you can guarantee same-direction in-flight IDs are unique (or same ID always to same port), drop the whole counter array → O(2^I) → O(I).

Module 4.3 (W deadlock, four criteria):

The Coffman deadlock conditions:
1. Mutual Exclusion
2. Hold and Wait
3. No Preemption
4. Circular Wait

In the W channel context:
1. Mutual Exclusion: At a mux, all W beats must arrive in the same order as AW beats regardless of the ID. So the mux's different slave ports exclude each other on the W channel — order fixed by AW arbiter. (Each mux picks one W stream at a time.)
2. Hold and Wait: AXI requires valid held high until ready (hold), while waiting for the other channel. W FIFO in mux holds a switching decision and waits for downstream.
3. No Preemption: AXI forbids W beat interleaving and requires W bursts in same order as AW — can't preempt/reorder.
4. Circular Wait: This is the one. Inserting a spill register between a demux master port and a mux slave port can create circular dependency inside the W FIFOs, due to how the rr_arbiter advances priority.

The example: an arbiter with 10 inputs; two requests in the same cycle; the higher priority wins and priority advances by one. Next cycle, same two requests again; the same port can win again (priority only advanced one position). Combined with the other arbiters in other muxes, this creates circular dependencies inside the FIFOs.

Solution: remove the spill register between demux and mux → forces the switching decision into the W FIFO in the same clock cycle → strict ordering of switching decisions → breaks circular wait.

    // FF to lock the AW valid signal, when a New arbitration decision is made the decision

input.content:
# 排序、停顿与无死锁设计

## 1. 本讲目标

本讲聚焦 `axi_xbar` 内部最容易被忽视、却决定整个互联「能不能用」的两个性质：**事务保序** 与 **无死锁**。读完本讲你应当能够：

- 说清 AXI「同 ID 同方向必须保序」这一约束，为什么会在交叉开关里被翻译成 **slave 端口停顿**；
- 解释 `AxiIdUsedSlvPorts`（即 demux 层的 `AxiLookBits`）如何在 **面积/延迟** 与 **误冲突（false conflict）** 之间折中；
- 复述 Coffman 死锁四条件，并论证为什么 **在 demux 输出与 mux 输入之间插入 spill 寄存器** 会同时满足四条件、从而在 W 通道引发死锁；
- 知道哪些旋钮（`FallThrough`、`lock_aw_valid`、`LatencyMode`）是**可以安全调整**的，哪些是**绝对不能动**的。

本讲不重复 u6-l1（xbar 怎么由 demux + mux 拼成）、u5-l2（id_counters 怎么计数）和 u5-l3（mux 怎么用 ID 高位路由），而是在它们之上回答「这些机制为什么这么设计、边界在哪里」。

## 2. 前置知识

- **in flight（在途）**：某接口上，事务的 `Ax` 地址拍已握手，但（最后一拍）响应尚未握手。详见 [doc/README.md:L26-L28](https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/blob/e55ae2a7ee606ee3cfd4257f63982a971b704407/doc/README.md#L26-L28)。
- **pending（挂起）**：某通道上 `valid` 为高而 `ready` 为低——一次「尚未发生的握手」。详见 [doc/README.md:L30-L32](https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/blob/e55ae2a7ee606ee3cfd4257f63982a971b704407/doc/README.md#L30-L32)。
- **保序（ordering）**：AXI 规定，同一个 master 发出的、**ID 相同且方向相同**（同为读或同为写）的事务，其响应必须按发起顺序返回给该 master。
- **reorder buffer（重排序缓冲）**：把乱序回来的响应按序重新排好再送出的硬件。本库的 xbar **没有**它，这是本讲一切取舍的源头。
- **Coffman 四条件**：操作系统课本里判定死锁的经典四条——互斥（Mutual Exclusion）、占有并等待（Hold and Wait）、不可抢占（No Preemption）、循环等待（Circular Wait）。四条同时成立 ⟹ 死锁。
- **spill 寄存器**：来自 `common_cells` 的原语，切断一条通道的组合路径，代价是一拍延迟。见 u4-l1 / u7-l1。

## 3. 本讲源码地图

| 文件 | 作用 |
|:--|:--|
| [doc/axi_xbar.md](https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/blob/e55ae2a7ee606ee3cfd4257f63982a971b704407/doc/axi_xbar.md) | xbar 的权威说明，含 *Ordering and Stalls*（保序与停顿）与 *Design Rationale for No Pipelining Inside Crossbar*（为何内部不流水）两节，是本讲的主线。 |
| [src/axi_demux.sv](https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/blob/e55ae2a7ee606ee3cfd4257f63982a971b704407/src/axi_demux.sv) | 1 拆 N 的路由器外壳，内含可配置的 spill 寄存器与对 `axi_demux_simple` 的例化。 |
| [src/axi_demux_simple.sv](https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/blob/e55ae2a7ee606ee3cfd4257f63982a971b704407/src/axi_demux_simple.sv) | demux 的组合内核，AW/AR 的「停顿门」、W 突发队列、id_counters 例化都在这里——本讲引用它来精确定位停顿条件。 |
| [src/axi_mux.sv](https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/blob/e55ae2a7ee606ee3cfd4257f63982a971b704407/src/axi_mux.sv) | N 合 1 的汇聚器，W FIFO、`rr_arb_tree` 仲裁器、`lock_aw_valid` 锁存都在这里——是分析 W 通道死锁的现场。 |
| [doc/axi_demux.md](https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/blob/e55ae2a7ee606ee3cfd4257f63982a971b704407/doc/axi_demux.md) | demux 文档，补充 `UniqueIds` 的面积复杂度结论 `O(2^I) → O(I)`。 |

## 4. 核心概念与源码讲解

### 4.1 同 ID 同方向保序：为什么会产生停顿

#### 4.1.1 概念说明

AXI 的排序模型规定：对同一个 master，**ID 相同、方向相同**（都读或都写）的事务，其响应必须按发起顺序返回。这是软件能看到的最强排序保证之一——例如 CPU 发了两条 `ID=5` 的读，它假定先发的那条数据先到。

现在把这个约束放进交叉开关：一个 slave 端口先后收到两个事务 `T1`、`T2`，二者 **ID 相同、方向相同**，但地址译码后要去往 **两个不同的 master 端口**（即两个物理上不同的下游 slave）。一旦 `T1`、`T2` 都放行，它们就进入了两个独立slave 的队列，谁先返回响应完全不可控——`T2` 的下游可能比 `T1` 的快，于是 `T2` 的响应先回到 slave 端口，**破坏了保序**。

修复办法只有两种：

1. **带 reorder buffer**：放行两个事务，等响应回来后重排。正确但贵。
2. **不带 reorder buffer**：干脆不让 `T2` 进来——`T1` 没完成前，停顿 `T2` 所在的 AW/AR 通道。

本库选了第 2 条，理由写在文档里：出于效率/面积考虑，**这个交叉开关没有 reorder buffer**。于是「保序」就被翻译成了「同 ID 跨端口的停顿」。

> 常见误解：停顿不是因为带宽不够，也不是因为 FIFO 满，而是 **纯粹为了满足 AXI 排序模型**。停顿期间下游可能完全空闲。

#### 4.1.2 核心流程

slave 端口收到一个新的 AW（或 AR）时，demux 的决策流程：

```text
新请求 (id=I, 方向=D, 目标端口=P_new)
   │
   ├─ 用 id_counters 查：ID=I、方向=D 当前是否已有在途事务？
   │     ├─ 否  ─► 放行，计数器 +1，登记端口 P_new
   │     └─ 是  ─► 在途事务登记的端口 P_old 是否 == P_new？
   │                ├─ 是 ─► 放行（同端口，天然保序，不破坏顺序）
   │                └─ 否 ─► 停顿：不拉 aw_ready/ar_ready，死等 P_old 那笔完成
   │
   └─ 待在途事务响应握手 ─► 计数器 -1；若减到 0，解除对 ID=I 的占用
```

关键点：**同 ID 去往同一端口不会停顿**（因为它们走同一条物理路径，下游自己就会保序）；**只有同 ID 去往不同端口才会停顿**。这就是 id_counters 必须记录「端口」而不仅仅是「计数」的原因。

#### 4.1.3 源码精读

文档把这条规则写得很直接（中文旁注：同 ID 同方向、不同 master 端口 ⟹ 第二笔要等第一笔完成，期间停顿 AW/AR；原因是库无 reorder buffer）：

> [doc/axi_xbar.md:L82-L87](https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/blob/e55ae2a7ee606ee3cfd4257f63982a971b704407/doc/axi_xbar.md#L82-L87) —— *Ordering and Stalls*：同 ID 同方向但去往不同 master 端口时，第二笔事务在前一笔完成前不被接收，期间 crossbar 停顿该 slave 端口的 AR/AW；原因是本 crossbar 没有 reorder buffer。

停顿在 RTL 里就是一个 `always_comb` 里的放行条件。AW 方向的放行门（中文旁注：第三行就是排序停顿门——ID 被占用且目标端口与在途端口不同则不进入 `if`，于是 `aw_valid` 保持 0、`aw_ready` 保持 0，即停顿）：

```systemverilog
// src/axi_demux_simple.sv:175-177  AW 放行条件
if (slv_req_i.aw_valid &&
      ((w_open == '0) || (w_select == slv_aw_select_i)) &&
      (!aw_select_occupied || (slv_aw_select_i == lookup_aw_select))) begin
```

- `aw_select_occupied`：ID 计数器报告「该 ID 已有在途写事务」；
- `lookup_aw_select`：该在途事务登记的目标端口；
- 仅当「未被占用」或「目标端口与在途端口一致」时才放行——这正是「同 ID 跨端口才停顿」的字面实现。

AR 方向有一个对称的、更简洁的门（中文旁注：读方向同样的停顿逻辑）：

```systemverilog
// src/axi_demux_simple.sv:325-326  AR 放行条件
if (slv_req_i.ar_valid && (!ar_select_occupied ||
   (slv_ar_select_i == lookup_ar_select))) begin
```

这套占用信息来自哪里？来自 u5-l2 讲过的 `axi_demux_id_counters`，AW/AR 各例化一份（中文旁注：AW 握手时 push、B 握手时 pop；push 时把目标端口 `slv_aw_select_i` 登记进计数器）：

> [src/axi_demux_simple.sv:L210-L229](https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/blob/e55ae2a7ee606ee3cfd4257f63982a971b704407/src/axi_demux_simple.sv#L210-L229) —— `i_aw_id_counter` 例化：`lookup_*` 查占用、`push_*` 登记、`pop_*` 回收。

> ⚠️ 注意区分：`w_open` / `w_select`（第 175 行第二行）管的是 **W 通道的突发队列**，避免不同端口的 W 拍交错（见 4.3）；它和 id_counters 的排序停顿是**两套独立机制**，不要混淆。

#### 4.1.4 代码实践（源码阅读型）

1. **目标**：在脑子里跑一次「同 ID 跨端口停顿」的场景，验证你对停顿触发条件的理解。
2. **步骤**：
   - 假设一个 2 master 端口的 demux，`slv_aw_select_i` 由地址最高位决定（地址 `< 0x8000_0000` 选端口 0，否则选端口 1）。
   - 设想 slave 端口按顺序到来两笔写：`T1 = (id=3, addr=0x0000)`、`T2 = (id=3, addr=0x9000_0000)`。注意 **ID 都是 3，但分别去端口 0 和端口 1**。
   - 打开 [src/axi_demux_simple.sv:L138-L194](https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/blob/e55ae2a7ee606ee3cfd4257f63982a971b704407/src/axi_demux_simple.sv#L138-L194) 的 AW `always_comb`，逐步代入 `T2` 时刻的状态：此时 `T1` 在途，`aw_select_occupied=1`、`lookup_aw_select=0`，而 `slv_aw_select_i=1`。
3. **需要观察的现象**：第 177 行的条件 `(!aw_select_occupied || (slv_aw_select_i == lookup_aw_select))` 求值为 `(!1 || (1==0)) = 0`，整个 `if` 不进入，`aw_valid` 与 `slv_resp_o.aw_ready` 都保持 0。
4. **预期结果**：`T2` 被钉在 slave 端口直到 `T1` 的 B 响应握手（pop 掉计数器、`aw_select_occupied` 归 0）。这正是「同 ID 跨端口停顿」。
5. 若要眼见为实，可在 `test/tb_axi_xbar.sv` 里用定向激励复现该场景并加 `$display` 观察 `aw_ready` 被压低若干拍——**待本地验证**。

#### 4.1.5 小练习与答案

**练习 1**：如果把 `T2` 的 ID 改成 4（与 `T1` 不同），还会停顿吗？
**答**：不会。不同 ID 之间没有保序约束，id_counters 查 `id=4` 发现未被占用，直接放行；两笔事务可在两个端口并发在途。

**练习 2**：假设 `T1`、`T2` 同 ID 且都去端口 0（地址都在低半区），会停顿吗？
**答**：不会。第 177 行 `slv_aw_select_i == lookup_aw_select` 成立（都是 0），放行；二者沿同一物理路径到同一下游，由下游自身保序。

**练习 3**：为什么本库宁可引入「跨端口停顿」也不加 reorder buffer？
**答**：reorder buffer 需要为每个在途 ID 缓存响应、维护顺序指针，面积与延迟随 ID 宽度和并发度爆炸；而「停顿」只需一组计数器，零额外存储。停顿只在「同 ID 跨端口」这种相对少见的模式下才发生，综合代价低得多。
